001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.bean.rfc7807;
018
019import java.net.*;
020import java.util.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
024
025/**
026 * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807">RFC 7807 &mdash; Problem Details for HTTP APIs</a>
027 * bean.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * Represents the canonical {@code application/problem+json} document. The five standard members defined by
031 * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">&sect;3.1</a> are modelled as typed
032 * properties; arbitrary <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.2">&sect;3.2</a>
033 * extension members flatten into the top-level JSON object via a {@link BeanProp @BeanProp("*")} triplet.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
036 * <p class='bjava'>
037 *    Problem <jv>problem</jv> = <jk>new</jk> Problem()
038 *       .setType(<jk>new</jk> URI(<js>"https://example.com/probs/out-of-credit"</js>))
039 *       .setTitle(<js>"You do not have enough credit."</js>)
040 *       .setStatus(403)
041 *       .setDetail(<js>"Your current balance is 30, but that costs 50."</js>)
042 *       .setInstance(<jk>new</jk> URI(<js>"/account/12345/msgs/abc"</js>))
043 *       .set(<js>"balance"</js>, 30)
044 *       .set(<js>"accounts"</js>, <jsm>list</jsm>(<js>"/account/12345"</js>, <js>"/account/67890"</js>));
045 *
046 *    String <jv>json</jv> = JsonSerializer.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.serialize(<jv>problem</jv>);
047 * </p>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
050 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
051 *    <li>The {@code type} field is nullable. {@link #getType()} returns the raw value (possibly {@code null});
052 *       {@link #getTypeOrDefault()} returns the spec default {@code "about:blank"} per
053 *       <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">RFC 7807 &sect;3.1</a> when the
054 *       field is unset. The field is never eagerly defaulted, so a fresh {@code Problem} round-trips without a
055 *       synthetic {@code "type":"about:blank"} on the wire.
056 *    <li>RFC 7807 was obsoleted by <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457">RFC 9457</a> in
057 *       July 2023, but the data model and the {@code application/problem+json} IANA registration are unchanged.
058 * </ul>
059 *
060 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
061 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauBeanRfc7807">juneau-bean-rfc7807</a>
062 * </ul>
063 */
064@Marshalled
065public class Problem {
066
067   /**
068    * The default {@code type} URI defined by
069    * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">RFC 7807 &sect;3.1</a> for problem
070    * documents that omit the {@code type} member.
071    */
072   public static final URI DEFAULT_TYPE = URI.create("about:blank");
073
074   private URI type;
075   private String title;
076   private Integer status;
077   private String detail;
078   private URI instance;
079   private Map<String,Object> extra;
080
081   /**
082    * Convenience factory for the common &quot;build a problem from a status code + title + detail&quot; call site.
083    *
084    * <p>
085    * Populates only the {@code status}, {@code title}, and {@code detail} members. Callers wire from
086    * {@link Throwable}s or HTTP exception types themselves &mdash; for example:
087    * <p class='bjava'>
088    *    Problem.<jsm>fromStatus</jsm>(<jv>e</jv>.getStatusCode(), <jv>e</jv>.getStatusLine().getReasonPhrase(), <jv>e</jv>.getMessage());
089    * </p>
090    *
091    * @param status The HTTP status code (e.g. {@code 404}).
092    * @param title A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
093    * @param detail A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
094    * @return A new {@link Problem} populated with the supplied values.
095    */
096   public static Problem fromStatus(int status, String title, String detail) {
097      return new Problem().setStatus(status).setTitle(title).setDetail(detail);
098   }
099
100   /**
101    * The <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">type</a> URI that identifies
102    * the problem type.
103    *
104    * <p>
105    * Returns the raw field value, which may be <jk>null</jk> when the {@code type} member is absent. Use
106    * {@link #getTypeOrDefault()} to get the spec default {@code "about:blank"} for absent values.
107    *
108    * @return The {@code type} URI, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
109    */
110   public URI getType() {
111      return type;
112   }
113
114   /**
115    * Returns the {@code type} URI, defaulting to {@link #DEFAULT_TYPE} ({@code "about:blank"}) per
116    * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">RFC 7807 &sect;3.1</a> when the
117    * field is <jk>null</jk>.
118    *
119    * <p>
120    * The underlying field is left untouched, so this method never causes a synthetic {@code "type":"about:blank"}
121    * to appear on the wire.
122    *
123    * @return The {@code type} URI, never <jk>null</jk>.
124    */
125   @BeanIgnore
126   public URI getTypeOrDefault() {
127      return type == null ? DEFAULT_TYPE : type;
128   }
129
130   /**
131    * Sets the {@code type} URI.
132    *
133    * @param value The new value. Can be <jk>null</jk> to unset the property.
134    * @return This object.
135    */
136   public Problem setType(URI value) {
137      type = value;
138      return this;
139   }
140
141   /**
142    * The <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">title</a> &mdash; a short,
143    * human-readable summary of the problem type.
144    *
145    * @return The title, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
146    */
147   public String getTitle() {
148      return title;
149   }
150
151   /**
152    * Sets the {@code title}.
153    *
154    * @param value The new value. Can be <jk>null</jk> to unset the property.
155    * @return This object.
156    */
157   public Problem setTitle(String value) {
158      title = value;
159      return this;
160   }
161
162   /**
163    * The <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">status</a> &mdash; the HTTP
164    * status code originally generated by the origin server for this occurrence.
165    *
166    * <p>
167    * Modelled as {@link Integer} (boxed) because RFC 7807 declares {@code status} as OPTIONAL.
168    *
169    * @return The status code, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
170    */
171   public Integer getStatus() {
172      return status;
173   }
174
175   /**
176    * Sets the {@code status} HTTP status code.
177    *
178    * @param value The new value. Can be <jk>null</jk> to unset the property.
179    * @return This object.
180    */
181   public Problem setStatus(Integer value) {
182      status = value;
183      return this;
184   }
185
186   /**
187    * The <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">detail</a> &mdash; a
188    * human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
189    *
190    * @return The detail, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
191    */
192   public String getDetail() {
193      return detail;
194   }
195
196   /**
197    * Sets the {@code detail}.
198    *
199    * @param value The new value. Can be <jk>null</jk> to unset the property.
200    * @return This object.
201    */
202   public Problem setDetail(String value) {
203      detail = value;
204      return this;
205   }
206
207   /**
208    * The <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.1">instance</a> URI that
209    * identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.
210    *
211    * @return The {@code instance} URI, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
212    */
213   public URI getInstance() {
214      return instance;
215   }
216
217   /**
218    * Sets the {@code instance} URI.
219    *
220    * @param value The new value. Can be <jk>null</jk> to unset the property.
221    * @return This object.
222    */
223   public Problem setInstance(URI value) {
224      instance = value;
225      return this;
226   }
227
228   /**
229    * Generic <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.2">extension-member</a>
230    * keyset.
231    *
232    * @return All extension keys on this problem. Never <jk>null</jk>.
233    */
234   @BeanProp("*")
235   public Set<String> extraKeys() {
236      return extra == null ? Collections.emptySet() : extra.keySet();
237   }
238
239   /**
240    * Generic extension-member getter.
241    *
242    * <p>
243    * Used by the bean framework to read arbitrary
244    * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.2">&sect;3.2</a> extension members
245    * that flatten into the top-level JSON object.
246    *
247    * @param property The property name to retrieve. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
248    * @return The property value, or <jk>null</jk> if the property does not exist.
249    */
250   @BeanProp("*")
251   public Object get(String property) {
252      return extra == null ? null : extra.get(property);
253   }
254
255   /**
256    * Generic extension-member setter.
257    *
258    * <p>
259    * Used by the bean framework to set arbitrary
260    * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807#section-3.2">&sect;3.2</a> extension members.
261    * The extension map is lazily initialised on first use.
262    *
263    * @param property The property name to set. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
264    * @param value The new value for the property.
265    * @return This object.
266    */
267   @BeanProp("*")
268   public Problem set(String property, Object value) {
269      if (extra == null)
270         extra = new LinkedHashMap<>();
271      extra.put(property, value);
272      return this;
273   }
274}
